moduleMaps.module: server.py (AutoCAD MCP Pro FastMCP 3.0 server, 2881 lines)
moduleMaps.purpose: Single-file FastMCP server that exposes the full AutoCAD automation surface: 108 @mcp.tool registrations, 6 @mcp.resource endpoints, and 5 @mcp.prompt templates, all dispatching to a dual-backend (COM live AutoCAD / ezdxf headless) singleton selected at startup. Tools are grouped into 13 numbered sections (1-12 plus a Premium meta-tools section 13, with sub-sections 8b/8c/8d). Each tool is a thin async wrapper that resolves the backend via _backend(ctx), calls a same-named backend method, and serializes dataclass returns via _dc().
public_surface: mcp = FastMCP(name='AutoCAD MCP Pro', instructions=..., lifespan=autocad_lifespan) [server.py:153]; middleware order added: ErrorHandlingMiddleware -> AuditMiddleware -> TimingMiddleware -> LoggingMiddleware [server.py:197-200]

autocad_lifespan(server) [server.py:107] — @lifespan-decorated; calls _make_backend(), yields {'backend': backend} or {'backend': None, 'init_error': str} on failure; logs unsafe-mode warning if dangerous_commands_enabled
_make_backend() [server.py:69] — reads AUTOCAD_MCP_BACKEND env (auto|com|ezdxf); Windows tries ComBackend then falls back to EzdxfBackend; non-Windows always ezdxf
_backend(ctx) [server.py:207] — pulls ctx.lifespan_context['backend']; raises ToolError('AutoCAD backend unavailable: <init_error>') if None
_dc(obj) [server.py:216] — recursive dataclass->dict via asdict; lists handled; passthrough otherwise
_registered_tool_count() [server.py:225] — counts mcp._local_provider._components keys starting 'tool:'; returns -1 on exception; used by system_status/system_about
AuditMiddleware.on_call_tool [server.py:135] — times every tool, logs TOOL OK / TOOL ERR

SECTION 1 Drawing Mgmt (11): drawing_info->drawing_info; drawing_new->drawing_new(+engineering.ensure_standard_linetypes/ensure_engineering_layers when bootstrap); drawing_open->drawing_open; drawing_save->drawing_save; drawing_save_as->drawing_save_as; drawing_export_dxf->drawing_export_dxf; drawing_export_pdf->drawing_export_pdf; drawing_purge->drawing_purge; drawing_audit->drawing_audit; drawing_close->drawing_close; drawing_undo->drawing_undo; drawing_redo->drawing_redo (NOTE: 12 tools present, not 11)

SECTION 2 Entity Creation (13): entity_create_line/circle/arc/polyline; entity_create_rectangle->entity_create_polyline (wrapper); entity_create_text/mtext/hatch/spline/ellipse/point/block_ref -> same-named backend method

SECTION 4 Entity Modification (10+4 corner): entity_move/copy/rotate/scale/mirror/offset; entity_trim/extend/fillet/chamfer (corner ops); entity_delete; entity_array_rectangular/polar; entity_set_properties -> same-named
SECTION 5 Entity Query (3): entity_get->entity_get; entity_list->entity_list; entity_delete_many [server.py:1065] -> loops backend.entity_delete per handle (server-side, has try/except per item)

SECTION 6 Layer (12+2): layer_list/create/delete/set_current/modify/freeze/thaw/lock/unlock/hide/show -> same-named; layer_isolate [server.py:1216] -> server-side loop over layer_list+layer_hide; linetype_list/linetype_load -> same-named

SECTION 7 Block (7): block_list/insert/explode/get_attributes/set_attributes/create_from_entities -> same-named; block_find_references [server.py:1362] -> backend.entity_list(type_filter='INSERT') filtered by properties['block_name'] server-side

SECTION 8 Analysis (8): analysis_entity_stats->analysis_stats; analysis_find_in_region->analysis_entities_in_region; analysis_measure_distance->analysis_measure_distance(+server angle calc); analysis_measure_area->analysis_measure_area(+server perimeter); analysis_bounding_box->analysis_bounding_box; analysis_select_by_layer->analysis_select_by_layer; analysis_select_by_type->analysis_select_by_type; analysis_layer_stats [server.py:1506] -> composes layer_list+entity_list server-side

SECTION 8b Batch (2): entity_batch_create [server.py:1538] -> dispatch dict to backend.entity_create_* ; entity_batch_modify [server.py:1590] -> dispatch to entity_move/rotate/scale/delete/set_properties
SECTION 8c Templates (2): template_apply_layers [server.py:1695] -> _LAYER_TEMPLATES + backend.layer_create loop; template_list [server.py:1730] -> pure in-memory
SECTION 8d Validation (1): validation_check [server.py:1749] -> composes layer_list+entity_list server-side

SECTION 9 View (4): view_zoom_extents->view_zoom_extents; view_zoom_window->view_zoom_window; view_screenshot->view_screenshot; view_zoom_and_screenshot->view_zoom_window/extents + view_screenshot (NOTE: 4 tools, CLAUDE.md says 5)

SECTION 12 Engineering (7): gear_draw_helical_front_view->engineering.draw_helical_gear_front_view; gear_draw_spur_front_view->draw_spur_gear_front_view; gear_draw_section_aa->draw_gear_section_aa; keyway_draw_keyed_bore->draw_keyed_bore; keyway_draw_section->draw_keyway_section; titleblock_apply_iso_a3->apply_iso_a3_titleblock; drawing_finalize->DrawingValidator().run + backend.drawing_save_as/view_screenshot

Entry point [server.py:2861]: manual argv parse for --transport/--port/--host; _validate_http_bind() refuses non-loopback bind unless ALLOW_REMOTE_HTTP + MCP_AUTH_TOKEN set
dependencies: fastmcp (FastMCP, Context, ToolError, lifespan, Middleware, ErrorHandlingMiddleware, LoggingMiddleware, TimingMiddleware, utilities.types.Image)

engineering package (draw_*_gear_*, draw_keyed_bore, draw_keyway_section, apply_iso_a3_titleblock, TitleBlockMetadata, DrawingValidator, ensure_engineering_layers, ensure_standard_linetypes) — titleblock/validator imports are guarded and fall back to None
stdlib: json, logging, math, os, sys, time, dataclasses.asdict, win32gui (optional, AutoCAD window detection)
key_flows: Startup: __main__ -> mcp.run() (stdio) or _validate_http_bind + mcp.run(transport,host,port) -> autocad_lifespan -> _make_backend() picks COM/ezdxf -> stored in lifespan_context['backend']
Per tool call: ErrorHandling -> Audit(timing) -> Timing -> Logging middleware -> tool fn -> _backend(ctx) -> backend method -> _dc() serialization

Premium production workflow (per CLAUDE.md): drawing_plan -> drawing_apply_iso_layers -> construction_xline -> entity_create_* -> entity_trim/fillet -> entity_select_smart -> dimension_auto -> drawing_critique (must be []) -> construction_clear -> drawing_finalize

Engineering workflow: drawing_new(bootstrap) -> titleblock_apply_iso_a3 -> gear_draw_*_front_view -> gear_draw_section_aa -> dimension_* -> drawing_finalize (8-step validator, raises ToolError on any error-severity finding)
Backend dispatch parity: server tools call identically-named backend methods; both EzdxfBackend and ComBackend declare all 8 premium meta-tool methods, but ComBackend's are NotImplementedError stubs

risks.title: All 8 Premium meta-tools are dead stubs on the COM backend (raise NotImplementedError)
risks.detail: ComBackend.drawing_plan/drawing_critique/point_from_snap/construction_xline/construction_clear/drawing_apply_iso_layers/dimension_auto/entity_select_smart all just `raise NotImplementedError("... pending Task #7/#8/#9")`. On Windows with AutoCAD running (the default COM backend), the entire Premium workflow that CLAUDE.md declares NON-NEGOTIABLE for production output is non-functional: drawing_plan fails immediately, so drawing_critique (replays the plan) and drawing_finalize's quality gate cannot work. The server instructions and CLAUDE.md present these as the mandatory path; on the primary/default backend they 500. This is a dispatch/parity bug, not just incompleteness — the tools are registered and advertised as working.

risks.title: drawing_finalize calls drawing_save_as with only one positional arg, relying on differing default formats per backend
risks.detail: `await backend.drawing_save_as(str(validated))` omits the fmt argument. EzdxfBackend default is fmt='dxf' (ezdxf_backend.py:345) but ComBackend default is fmt='dwg' (com_backend.py:515). So drawing_finalize(save_path='x.dwg') on ezdxf will try to write DXF logic to a .dwg path, and the format is silently backend-dependent rather than derived from the path extension. The save_as MCP tool itself defaults to 'dwg' (server.py:336), creating a third inconsistency. Likely produces wrong-format or mislabeled output files.

risks.title: drawing_new merges backend dict but engineering bootstrap can leave result without standard keys; non-dict raw silently wrapped
risks.detail: `raw = await backend.drawing_new(template)`; if raw is not a dict it is wrapped as {'result': raw}. If a backend ever returns a non-dict (e.g. a dataclass), callers expecting drawing metadata keys get only {'result': ...} plus 'bootstrap'. Bootstrap failures are swallowed into result['bootstrap']={'error':...} and reported as success (no ToolError), so a fully un-bootstrapped drawing looks like a normal success to the client even though CLAUDE.md treats the engineering layer scaffold as a precondition for gear_*/titleblock_* tools.

risks.title: system_about hardcodes a tool_groups catalog that has drifted from actual registrations
risks.detail: The static tool_groups dict omits registered tools: entity_query lists only ['entity_get','entity_list'] but entity_delete_many is also registered (it is misfiled under entity_creation at line 2079). 'view' lists 4 tools but the count and grouping don't reflect that the dict is maintained by hand. There is no engineering (gear_*/keyway_*/titleblock_*/drawing_finalize) group and no premium (drawing_plan/critique/point_from_snap/construction_*/dimension_auto/entity_select_smart) group at all — 15 registered tools are entirely absent from system_about's self-description, so an agent inspecting capabilities via system_about will not see the very tools CLAUDE.md mandates.

risks.title: Engineering titleblock/validator symbols may be None at call time (guarded import), causing opaque failures
risks.detail: TitleBlockMetadata/apply_iso_a3_titleblock and DrawingValidator are imported in try/except ImportError that sets them to None. titleblock_apply_iso_a3 (server.py:2266) does `from engineering import apply_iso_a3_titleblock, TitleBlockMetadata` then calls them; if the underlying module failed to import, these are None and the call raises 'NoneType is not callable' rather than a clear ToolError. Same for DrawingValidator in drawing_finalize (server.py:2294,2311). The tool registers fine and only fails at invocation with an unhelpful message.

risks.title: _registered_tool_count reaches into FastMCP private internals
risks.detail: Uses mcp._local_provider._components and string-prefix 'tool:' matching. Both are undocumented private attributes; a FastMCP minor upgrade can silently change them, making system_status/system_about report tool_count=-1 (the except fallback). Since CLAUDE.md says the count must come from here dynamically, a -1 would surface to clients as the tool count.

risks.title: view_zoom_extents/view_zoom_window registered for all backends but documented COM-only / no-op in ezdxf
risks.detail: CLAUDE.md states view ops are no-ops in ezdxf. The tools are unconditionally registered and call backend.view_zoom_extents/window; on ezdxf these presumably return a no-op success, so an agent gets a success response implying the view changed when it did not. view_zoom_and_screenshot (server.py:1870) relies on these before screenshotting; on ezdxf the zoom_window path may not actually frame the requested region. Verify ezdxf view ops actually affect the matplotlib render extents.

smells.title: Hardcoded/contradictory tool count: CLAUDE.md says 87 tools, file actually has 108 @mcp.tool registrations
smells.detail: grep ^@mcp.tool = 108 registrations. CLAUDE.md project overview says '87 tools, 6 resources, and 5 prompt templates' and instructs never to hardcode the count. The code correctly computes it dynamically (_registered_tool_count) but the docs/section headers are stale: Section 1 header says '(11 tools)' but 12 drawing_* tools exist (drawing_redo at 426 is the 12th); Section 9 header says '(5 tools)' but only 4 view tools are registered. Doc-vs-code drift across multiple section banners.

smells.title: Six near-identical @mcp.resource bodies — copy-paste backend-None guard + try/except json.dumps
smells.detail: resource_drawing_info, resource_layers, resource_blocks, resource_entity_stats, resource_status, resource_entities_by_layer all repeat the same pattern: fetch backend from lifespan_context, `if b is None: return json.dumps({'error':'Backend not ready'})`, try/except -> json.dumps(..., indent=2). Could be a single decorator/helper. Note they intentionally do NOT use _backend(ctx) (which would raise ToolError) — divergent error-handling convention between tools and resources.

smells.title: Five prompt templates duplicate the layer-set definitions that already live in _LAYER_TEMPLATES
smells.detail: prompt_floor_plan / prompt_mechanical_drawing / prompt_pid_diagram / prompt_electrical_schematic embed hardcoded layer lists (names, colors, lineweights) that overlap with the _LAYER_TEMPLATES dict (architectural/mechanical/electrical/piping). Two sources of truth for the 'standard' layer sets; they can drift (e.g. prompt mechanical uses VISIBLE/HIDDEN/CENTER with linetypes, _LAYER_TEMPLATES mechanical uses all Continuous linetypes).

smells.title: Section comment '(Task #7/#8/#9 implement these)' is a leftover TODO marker shipped in code
smells.detail: Comment '# ── premium meta-tools (Task #7/#8/#9 implement these) ──' plus 8 NotImplementedError messages referencing 'pending Task #7/#8/#9'. These are in-flight development markers left in the committed COM backend; combined with the abstract base + ezdxf having real implementations, this is clear unfinished-work drift that the tool registrations and instructions hide.

smells.title: Two unrelated comments reference other 'Agents'/Tasks indicating multi-agent build seams left in
smells.detail: Comment 'Agent B contributes titleblock + validator. Imports are guarded so this package remains usable while B is still in flight; once those modules land the symbols flow through transparently.' This, together with the com_backend Task #7/#8/#9 markers, shows the codebase was assembled by parallel agents and the seams (guarded imports to None, NotImplementedError stubs) were never reconciled before being presented as a finished 'production-grade' surface.

smells.title: block_find_references and analysis tools silently truncate / filter server-side without backend support
smells.detail: block_find_references calls backend.entity_list(type_filter='INSERT') with no limit, then filters in Python on e.properties.get('block_name'). entity_list's own MCP wrapper caps at max_list_limit, but this server-side call bypasses that cap and could pull unbounded INSERTs into memory; also depends on every backend populating properties['block_name'] identically (parity assumption not enforced by the ABC).

smells.title: Manual argv parsing in __main__ instead of using fastmcp's CLI / argparse
smells.detail: --transport/--port/--host are parsed by hand via args.index(); no validation that the flag has a following value (IndexError if '--port' is last arg), no --help, and duplicates what `fastmcp run server.py:mcp` already provides (the module docstring even recommends the fastmcp CLI). Fragile and redundant.

moduleMaps.notes: Counts verified by grep: 108 @mcp.tool, 6 @mcp.resource, 5 @mcp.prompt. Backend parity confirmed: both backends implement every abstract method including the 8 premium meta-tools and 4 corner ops — but ComBackend's 8 premium methods are NotImplementedError stubs (com_backend.py:1637-1660), while EzdxfBackend has full implementations (ezdxf_backend.py:1753-2008). No tool dispatches to a method missing from a backend signature; the gap is stub-vs-real, which is worse because it passes registration and ABC checks. Engineering tools (Section 12) and Premium tools (Section 13) push the real total well above the documented 87. The 12-section taxonomy in CLAUDE.md is incomplete: actual sections are 1-12 plus 8b/8c/8d (batch/templates/validation) and 13 (premium) = 6 extra tool groups not in the canonical list.

moduleMaps.purpose: Defines the abstract interface every AutoCAD backend must implement plus the five shared serialization dataclasses (EntityInfo, LayerInfo, BlockInfo, DrawingInfo, CommandResult). It is the single source of truth for the strategy-pattern dual-backend architecture (EzdxfBackend, ComBackend). Two methods are concrete (set_layer_active, ensure_linetypes); the rest (~73) are @abstractmethod. Also exports three free helpers: shoelace_area, deg2rad, rad2deg.
public_surface: dataclass CommandResult(ok: bool, payload: Any = None, error: str|None = None) + to_dict() (base.py:26-38)
dataclass EntityInfo(handle, type, layer, color, linetype, visible, properties: dict = {}) (base.py:41-49)
dataclass LayerInfo(name, color, linetype, lineweight, is_on, is_frozen, is_locked, is_current) (base.py:52-61)
dataclass BlockInfo(name, origin: tuple[float,float], attribute_count, entity_count, is_xref, description: str = '') (base.py:64-71)
dataclass DrawingInfo(name, full_path, saved, entity_count, layer_count, block_count, extents_min, extents_max, units, version='', backend='') (base.py:74-86)
async drawing_save_as(path: str, fmt: str='dwg') -> dict (base.py:125)
free fn shoelace_area(points) -> float (base.py:626); deg2rad (base.py:639); rad2deg (base.py:643)
dependencies: engineering.plan_spec (TYPE_CHECKING-only: CritiqueFocus, DimStyle, Issue, LayerSetId, PlanSpec, SheetSize, SnapType) — string-annotated, runtime-imported inside ezdxf_backend methods
key_flows: server.py::_make_backend instantiates EzdxfBackend or ComBackend (both subclass AutoCADBackend); the singleton is stored in lifespan context and every @mcp.tool calls _backend(ctx).<method>(...).
Dataclass returns (EntityInfo/LayerInfo/BlockInfo/DrawingInfo) flow back through server.py::_dc() which converts them to dicts for JSON; CommandResult has its own to_dict().

Premium meta-tools (drawing_plan/critique/point_from_snap/construction_*/drawing_apply_iso_layers/dimension_auto/entity_select_smart) are part of the contract and resolve engineering.plan_spec / engineering.critique at call time inside the ezdxf implementation.
ensure_linetypes (concrete) drives backend-specific linetype_list/linetype_load via the contract methods; used by the engineering scaffold bootstrap.

risks.title: COM backend stubs ALL 8 premium meta-tools with NotImplementedError — contract violation
risks.detail: drawing_plan, drawing_critique, point_from_snap, construction_xline, construction_clear, drawing_apply_iso_layers, dimension_auto, and entity_select_smart each `raise NotImplementedError("... pending Task #7/#8/#9")`. EzdxfBackend fully implements all 8 (ezdxf_backend.py:1753-2060). CLAUDE.md declares the premium workflow 'non-negotiable for production engineering output', yet on Windows the default backend is COM (server._make_backend tries COM first). Result: drawing_plan/drawing_apply_iso_layers/drawing_critique/dimension_auto and the mandated finalize gate are dead on the very platform the project targets — every premium-workflow tool call crashes with NotImplementedError surfacing as a 500/ToolError. This is the single largest contract-vs-implementation divergence in the repo.

risks.title: drawing_save_as default `fmt` differs from the contract (dwg vs dxf)
risks.detail: ABC signature is `drawing_save_as(self, path: str, fmt: str = 'dwg')` (base.py:126); ComBackend matches with `fmt='dwg'` (com_backend.py:515) but EzdxfBackend declares `fmt: str = 'dxf'` (ezdxf_backend.py:345). A caller relying on the documented default gets DWG on COM and DXF on ezdxf for the same call — a silent cross-backend parity/format divergence. (Arguably ezdxf cannot produce real DWG, so the override is intentional, but it still breaks the contract default and is undocumented.)

risks.title: Array tools: original-entity inclusion differs between backends (count parity)
risks.detail: EzdxfBackend.entity_array_rectangular explicitly `continue`s the (0,0) cell (ezdxf_backend.py:927-928) and entity_array_polar iterates `range(1,count)` (ezdxf_backend.py:947), so it returns rows*cols-1 / count-1 NEW entities (original excluded). The COM path calls native ArrayRectangular/ArrayPolar (com_backend.py:893,920) and returns whatever COM yields, with an except-branch that, on iteration failure, appends a single `_entity_info(result)` (com_backend.py:903,929). The returned list length and whether the original is represented are not guaranteed equal across backends for identical inputs — clients counting/positioning by list index can break when switching backends.

risks.title: entity_extend auto-endpoint fallback can extend toward origin on COM
risks.detail: When end_x/end_y are None and reading StartPoint/EndPoint raises, the except clause sets `ex, ey = 0.0, 0.0` (com_backend.py:1580-1581) and then issues `_EXTEND ... 0,0`, picking the drawing origin as the pick point. For geometry far from origin this extends/selects the wrong segment silently instead of failing. The contract (base.py:319-322) promises 'the target endpoint nearest the boundary is auto-selected' — the silent (0,0) fallback violates that promise.

risks.title: point_from_snap 'int' (intersection) snap declared in contract but support unverified per type
risks.detail: The docstring enumerates snap ∈ {end, mid, center, quad, int, perp, near} (base.py:540) and SnapType is a closed type. The ezdxf _sync handler (ezdxf_backend.py:1778+) branches on snap strings; I read through line 1812 (end/arc handling) but did not confirm every enum member — notably 'int' (intersection, which needs a second entity but the signature only takes one handle + ref_x/ref_y). If 'int' falls through to a generic else it may raise or mis-snap. Verify the full branch list covers all 7 SnapType members and that 'int'/'perp' semantics match the single-handle signature.

smells.title: BlockInfo.description field is never populated by either backend
smells.detail: BlockInfo declares `description: str = ''` (base.py:71) but neither block_list constructs BlockInfo with a description — ezdxf (ezdxf_backend.py:1195) and COM (com_backend.py:1185) both omit the kwarg, so it is always ''. ezdxf block definitions expose `blk.block.dxf.description`; the field is dead/unused as currently wired.

smells.title: Premium meta-tool stubs reference internal task IDs in user-facing errors
smells.detail: Each NotImplementedError message embeds 'pending Task #7/#8/#9' (e.g. com_backend.py:1639,1645,1653). These internal planning-ticket references leak into runtime error payloads surfaced to MCP clients; they convey nothing actionable to an end user and are doc-vs-code drift once the tasks are 'done' on the ezdxf side but not COM.

smells.title: drawing_redo / block_create_from_entities are contract-conformant but inert on respective backends
smells.detail: EzdxfBackend.drawing_redo always returns {'ok': False, 'error': 'Redo not supported in ezdxf backend'} (ezdxf_backend.py:487) and ComBackend.block_create_from_entities always returns {'ok': False, ...'not supported in COM backend'...} (com_backend.py:1256-1260). These satisfy the abstract signature shape but are permanently no-op, creating asymmetric capability across backends not flagged in base.py — silent feature gaps a caller can only discover at runtime.

smells.title: _v2 nested helper redefined per-call inside _entity_info_dxf
smells.detail: `_v2` (Vec3/tuple -> [x,y]) is defined as a closure inside _entity_info_dxf on every entity conversion (ezdxf_backend.py:122). Minor allocation/readability smell — it has no closure dependency and could be a module-level function reused across the file (COM has an analogous standalone pattern). Not a correctness issue.

smells.title: LayerInfo.lineweight default sentinel (-3) leaks raw ACAD enum into a float field
smells.detail: layer_create defaults lineweight=-3 (base.py:377) and _layer_info_dxf passes through `lineweight` defaulting to -3 (ezdxf_backend.py:184) as a float. -3 is AutoCAD's LWDEFAULT magic constant, not a millimetre value, but the dataclass field is typed `lineweight: float` (base.py:57) with no documentation that negative values are sentinels (-1 ByLayer, -2 ByBlock, -3 Default). Consumers doing arithmetic/ISO-128 validation on lineweight (the CLAUDE.md ISO-128 rule) can misread the sentinel as a real 0.x mm value.

moduleMaps.notes: Coverage cross-check: I grepped every `def`/`async def` in both backends (ezdxf_backend.py and com_backend.py) against the ~73 abstract methods. Both backends define a method body for EVERY abstract method (no missing-override AttributeError risk), so all subclasses instantiate. The contract violations are behavioral, not structural: COM stubs the 8 premium meta-tools with NotImplementedError (critical, since COM is the default Windows backend), and several methods are inert no-ops (drawing_redo on ezdxf, block_create_from_entities on COM). I verified dimension_* (5), corner ops (trim/extend/fillet/chamfer), arrays, block_list, and the dataclass constructors directly. NOT fully read: the complete point_from_snap branch table beyond line 1812, engineering/plan_spec.py, engineering/critique.py, and server.py tool registrations — the 'int'/perp snap risk and dimension_auto internals should be re-verified against those files in a follow-up pass.

moduleMaps.purpose: Headless, file-based implementation of the AutoCADBackend ABC using the ezdxf library. Provides DXF create/modify/query/analysis/dimension/block/layer/linetype operations plus the "premium" meta-tools (plan/critique/snap/construction/iso-layers/auto-dim/smart-select) and corner ops (trim/extend/fillet/chamfer). All synchronous ezdxf work is offloaded to threads via _async(); transactions are implemented as full on-disk DXF snapshots pushed onto an _undo_stack. Runs anywhere (no AutoCAD/COM required); view zoom and system run-command/run-lisp are documented no-ops.
public_surface: premium: drawing_plan/critique, point_from_snap, construction_xline/clear, drawing_apply_iso_layers, dimension_auto, entity_select_smart (1753-2050)
dependencies: ezdxf (core: new/readfile, layouts, math.Matrix44/Vec2/Vec3, bbox, tools.standards, addons.drawing matplotlib Frontend/RenderContext)
engineering.plan_spec.PlanSpec, engineering.critique.run_critique, engineering.layers.apply_layer_set (lazy-imported in premium meta-tools)
stdlib: asyncio, io, math, os, tempfile, pathlib
key_flows: Create: tool -> entity_create_* -> _msp().add_* -> _apply_attrs(layer/color/linetype) -> _mark_dirty() -> _entity_info_dxf() returns handle; wrapped by _async (lock + to_thread).
Modify/query: handle -> _get_entity(handle) via doc.entitydb.get(handle) -> mutate via .transform()/.translate() or read .dxf.* attrs.

Transactions/undo: transaction_begin saves full doc.saveas(tmpfile) onto _undo_stack (trimmed to max_undo_stack); rollback/undo do ezdxf.readfile(snapshot) replacing self._doc; commit just unlinks top snapshot.
Linetype resolution: _ensure_linetype_loaded -> ezdxf.tools.standards -> _AUTOCAD_FALLBACK_LINETYPES -> warn-and-continue (renders Continuous).
Screenshot/PDF: build matplotlib figure via ezdxf addons.drawing Frontend/RenderContext, savefig to BytesIO/path; ImportError -> None (screenshot) or RuntimeError (pdf).
Premium auto-dim: dimension_auto gathers LINE endpoints then emits dimension_linear calls per chain/baseline/ordinate style on layer DIM.

risks.title: drawing_undo / transaction_rollback reload doc but do NOT reset _doc_path or _current_layer
risks.detail: Both replace self._doc via ezdxf.readfile(snapshot) but leave self._doc_path and self._current_layer unchanged, and force self._dirty=True. If the snapshot was taken before a drawing_save_as that changed _doc_path, the in-memory path now disagrees with the reloaded content. More importantly drawing_undo/rollback share the SAME _undo_stack with transaction_begin: a user-level transaction snapshot and an undo checkpoint are indistinguishable, so transaction_commit/rollback can silently pop a snapshot created for a different purpose (no transaction-id or depth separation).

risks.title: entity_offset ignores side_x/side_y; offset side is not controllable and ARC/LWPOLYLINE/ELLIPSE unsupported
risks.detail: The signature accepts side_x/side_y but they are never read. LINE offset direction is hard-fixed to normal=(-dir.y, dir.x)*distance, so the caller cannot choose which side; only the sign of `distance` flips it. CIRCLE offset is radius+distance (no side either). All other types (ARC, LWPOLYLINE, ELLIPSE, SPLINE) raise RuntimeError('Offset not supported'), i.e. silently-unsupported for the common polyline-offset case.

risks.title: dimension_angular ignores text-location params tx,ty and uses hardcoded distance=10.0
risks.detail: Signature is (vx,vy,x1,y1,x2,y2,tx,ty,layer) but tx/ty are never used; add_angular_dim_2l is called with a fixed distance=10.0 regardless of the requested text/arc location. The dimension arc radius is therefore always 10 drawing-units from the vertex, which for large or small parts places the arc inside/outside the geometry. COM parity is broken (COM presumably honors tx/ty).

risks.title: entity_array_polar double-places at full 360deg fill (overlap with original)
risks.detail: step = radians(fill_angle)/max(count-1,1) and the loop runs i in range(1,count). For a full circle fill_angle=360 the inclusive-endpoint divisor count-1 makes the last copy land at exactly 360deg == 0deg, coinciding with the original entity (duplicate). AutoCAD's polar array divides a 360 fill by count (not count-1). For partial fills count-1 is correct, so the bug is specifically the closed/full-circle case — a very common gear/bolt-circle pattern, which the engineering layer relies on.

risks.title: entity_copy/mirror/array add a raw .copy() to modelspace, relying on add_entity to assign a fresh handle
risks.detail: ent.copy() in ezdxf produces an unbound entity that still carries the source handle until add_entity rebinds it. The code calls add_entity(copy) BEFORE reading copy.dxf.handle (in array/explode) and before translate, which is the correct order — but it is fragile: any reordering, or an ezdxf version where copy() retains the handle and add_entity does not reassign, would yield duplicate handles silently. No assertion that the new handle differs from the source.

risks.title: Broad except Exception: pass swallows failures in purge and block_create_from_entities
risks.detail: drawing_purge silently swallows every delete failure (blocks/layers/linetypes/styles) with bare 'except Exception: pass', so a layer that fails to purge is counted as not-purged with no diagnostic. block_create_from_entities swallows per-handle add failures at debug level, so a block can be created reporting entity_count lower than requested with no error surfaced to the caller.

risks.title: view_screenshot/export_pdf catch only ImportError; runtime render errors propagate or are inconsistent
risks.detail: Both wrap the matplotlib render in try/except ImportError only. A non-import rendering failure (e.g. unsupported entity, empty modelspace, savefig OSError) propagates as a raw exception out of the threaded _sync for view_screenshot (which is documented to return bytes|None), and for export_pdf too. Also fig is not closed on the non-Import exception path, leaking matplotlib figures across repeated failing calls.

risks.title: _apply_attrs auto-creates layers but does NOT ensure the entity's linetype is loaded
risks.detail: entity_create_* -> _apply_attrs sets entity.dxf.linetype = linetype directly without calling _ensure_linetype_loaded (unlike entity_set_properties:980 and layer_modify:1074 which do). Creating a line with linetype='HIDDEN' assigns the name but never adds the linetype table entry, so ezdxf may raise on save or the line renders Continuous. Inconsistent with the documented 'write linetype="CENTER" without separately loading it' contract.

risks.title: drawing_save_as ignores fmt parameter (always writes DXF via saveas)
risks.detail: fmt is accepted and echoed back in the result but doc.saveas(path) only ever writes the format inferred from extension/DXF. A caller passing fmt='dwg' gets {'format':'dwg'} but a DXF file on disk — silent format mismatch. ezdxf cannot write DWG; this should error rather than claim success.

risks.title: Concurrency: every op serialized on a single asyncio.Lock; commit does file I/O outside the lock
risks.detail: _async holds self._lock around the to_thread call, so all backend operations are fully serialized (no parallelism — acceptable for a single doc but defeats the thread pool). transaction_commit runs p.unlink() directly on the event loop thread WITHOUT _async/lock, racing with any concurrent _async op that touches _undo_stack (e.g. transaction_begin trimming the stack). The list mutation of _undo_stack is not lock-protected in commit.

risks.title: block_list base_point/origin indexing assumes 3-tuple; entities_in_region fallback uses only insert/start
risks.detail: block_list reads origin[0]/origin[1] from dxf.get('base_point', default) — fine for the default but if base_point is a Vec3 subscripting may differ across ezdxf versions. analysis_entities_in_region's non-bbox fallback only inspects insert/start points, so circles/arcs/text-by-center and any entity without .insert/.start are silently excluded from region selection when _BBOX_OK is False.

smells.title: V1 placeholder limitations advertised in error strings (LINE-only trim/extend/fillet/chamfer/dimension_auto)
smells.detail: trim/extend/fillet/chamfer all raise 'V1 supports LINE+LINE only ... V2 will add LINE+ARC'; dimension_auto raises 'V1: only LINE entities supported'; point_from_snap 'int' raises 'V2 will accept a 2nd handle'. These are functional gaps shipped as runtime errors. ARC/CIRCLE/LWPOLYLINE corner ops — core CAD operations — are unimplemented despite the premium workflow docs implying fillet/chamfer are first-class.

smells.title: Unused imports / accepted-but-ignored parameters
smells.detail: colors, units, TextEntityAlignment, BSpline, Vec3 imported with noqa but unused in this module. _fillet_chamfer_setup returns A_far/B_far which entity_fillet (1660) and entity_chamfer (1723) unpack but never use. dimension_angular tx/ty and entity_offset side_x/side_y are dead parameters (see risks).

smells.title: entity_create_mtext: rotation cannot be set; width/height defaults baked into module not config
smells.detail: MTEXT has no rotation parameter (TEXT does at 560-576), so rotated multi-line notes are impossible via this backend. The COM backend may support rotation, creating cross-backend behavior divergence for the same MCP tool.

smells.title: Duplicated handle-nearest-endpoint trim logic copy-pasted across fillet, chamfer, trim, extend
smells.detail: The 'if _dist2(start,P) <= _dist2(end,P): set start else set end' block is duplicated 6+ times across entity_trim, entity_fillet, entity_chamfer. Should be a shared helper (e.g. _shorten_line_to(line, P, new_pt)) to avoid divergent edits.

smells.title: drawing_undo and transaction_rollback are near-identical duplicate implementations
smells.detail: Both pop _undo_stack, ezdxf.readfile the snapshot into self._doc, set _dirty=True, and unlink the temp file. Same body, two methods sharing the same stack — confirms the conceptual conflation of 'undo history' and 'transactions' noted in risks. Should delegate to one private helper.

smells.title: Magic constants: angular dim distance=10.0, screenshot figsize 16x9 dpi 100, pdf dpi 150, construction color 250
smells.detail: Hardcoded render/geometry constants not surfaced through config.settings. The angular-dim 10.0 is a correctness issue (see risk); the dpi/figsize/color are tuning knobs that differ from the COM backend's capture path and produce non-matching screenshots between backends.

smells.title: entity_create_text rotation passed straight through; no angle normalization vs AutoCAD CCW-from-X convention
smells.detail: rotation is forwarded as-is to dxfattribs['rotation']. ezdxf TEXT rotation is degrees CCW which matches the documented convention, so this is OK, but there is no validation/normalization and _entity_info_dxf reads rotation back via ent.dxf.get('rotation',0.0) — consistent, just noting no guard for radians-vs-degrees caller mistakes.

smells.title: system_set_variable writes arbitrary header vars with no validation/coercion
smells.detail: doc.header[f'${name.upper()}'] = value with no type check; passing a string '4' for $INSUNITS (which drawing_info reads as an int key into unit_map) would store a string and break the unit_map.get lookup. The CLAUDE.md prompt even shows users calling system_set_variable('INSUNITS', 4) — a wrong-typed value silently corrupts the header var.

moduleMaps.notes: Read the full file (lines 1-2050) plus config.py (max_undo_stack=5, max_dxf_bytes=50MB) and base.py signatures, and confirmed in server.py that engineering-layer bootstrap lives in the server tool (drawing_new in this backend correctly does NOT bootstrap, so no doc drift there). _async() correctness: it acquires self._lock then asyncio.to_thread — fully serializing the single doc (intended for STA-like safety, but defeats the thread pool's purpose since only one op runs at a time). Transaction model is full-DXF-snapshot-to-tempfile on _undo_stack, trimmed to max_undo_stack=5; this means every transaction_begin does a complete document serialization to disk — O(drawing size) per call and a real perf/memory concern for large drawings (the snapshots also persist on disk between calls). Documented no-ops confirmed: view_zoom_extents/window (1394-1398) and system_run_command/run_lisp (1510-1520) return ok/error dicts without doing work, matching CLAUDE.md. Silently-unsupported entity types found: entity_offset (only LINE/CIRCLE), corner ops + dimension_auto + point_from_snap-int (LINE-only), entities_in_region fallback (insert/start only). The most impactful correctness bugs to verify with tests: (1) entity_array_polar full-360 duplicate, (2) dimension_angular ignoring tx/ty + fixed distance, (3) entity_offset ignoring requested side, (4) _apply_attrs not loading linetypes on create.

moduleMaps.purpose: Implements the AutoCADBackend ABC against a live AutoCAD instance via pywin32 COM. All COM access is serialized through a single-thread STA ThreadPoolExecutor (one worker, CoInitialize'd) wrapped by async def _run, which applies a hard per-call timeout (config.settings.com_call_timeout, default 60s) and rebuilds the executor + drops the cached app on timeout/fatal HRESULT. Provides entity create/modify/query, layers, linetypes, blocks, dimensions, analysis (UUID-named SelectionSets), Win32 PrintWindow screenshots, transactions (AutoCAD undo marks), and SendCommand-based run_command/run_lisp + corner ops (trim/extend/fillet/chamfer via _safe_send_command polling CMDACTIVE).
public_surface: ComBackend._run(func,*args) — STA executor dispatch + timeout/HRESULT recovery (com_backend.py:397)
entity_create_* / entity_* modify / dimension_* / layer_* / block_* / analysis_* / view_* / transaction_* / system_* tool methods

8 premium meta-tools (drawing_plan, drawing_critique, point_from_snap, construction_xline, construction_clear, drawing_apply_iso_layers, dimension_auto, entity_select_smart) — all raise NotImplementedError (com_backend.py:1637-1660)
dependencies: pywin32 (pythoncom, pywintypes, win32com.client, win32con, win32gui, win32ui)
asyncio, concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor, uuid, math, io, time, logging
key_flows: Async tool call → ComBackend.method() builds inner _sync() closure → await self._run(_sync) → loop.run_in_executor(single STA thread) → asyncio.wait_for(timeout) → COM call on worker thread → dataclass result
Connect: connect() only builds the ThreadPoolExecutor (initializer=_com_init→CoInitialize); actual AutoCAD GetActiveObject/Dispatch is lazy on first _acad_app() call inside the worker thread
Timeout recovery: asyncio.TimeoutError → abandon stuck executor via shutdown(wait=False), build fresh single-thread executor, pop cached _COM_STATE['app'], set _connected=False, raise RuntimeError
HRESULT recovery: pywintypes.com_error with hr in {RPC_E_DISCONNECTED, server-unavailable, busy} → drop cached app + _connected=False, re-raise RuntimeError
Selection-set flow (analysis_entities_in_region / analysis_select_by_layer): SelectionSets.Add(UUID name) → select → iterate → finally ss.Delete()

Corner ops: _safe_send_command zeros OSMODE/SNAPMODE/CMDECHO, snapshots ModelSpace handles, SendCommand, busy-polls CMDACTIVE with time.sleep(0.05) until 0 or deadline, restores vars, returns new handles
Screenshot: view_screenshot → _find_autocad_hwnd (EnumWindows title contains 'AutoCAD') → _capture_window GDI PrintWindow(PW_RENDERFULLCONTENT) → PIL BGRX→PNG

risks.title: Timeout abandons STA worker thread permanently while it stays blocked inside SendCommand, leaking a CoInitialize'd thread per timeout
risks.detail: On asyncio.TimeoutError the code does stuck.shutdown(wait=False) and builds a fresh executor. The old worker thread is still blocked inside a synchronous COM/SendCommand call and cannot be cancelled by Python; shutdown(wait=False) does NOT kill it. Each timeout therefore permanently leaks one OS thread that holds an apartment (CoInitialize was called, CoUninitialize never is) plus a COM proxy to AutoCAD. Repeated timeouts (e.g. a modal dialog left open) accumulate zombie threads and COM references against the single AutoCAD process. There is no upper bound and no CoUninitialize anywhere in the file.

risks.title: _com_init never calls CoUninitialize; abandoned/replaced executor threads leak the COM apartment
risks.detail: _com_init calls pythoncom.CoInitialize() in the worker thread but no finalizer/atexit ever calls pythoncom.CoUninitialize(). disconnect() does executor.shutdown(wait=False) (does not block, does not run any per-thread teardown). Combined with the timeout-rebuild path (line 423), every replaced executor leaves a thread that initialized COM but never uninitialized it.

risks.title: _safe_send_command busy-polls CMDACTIVE with time.sleep on the single STA thread, monopolizing the executor for up to deadline_s and serializing all other tool calls
risks.detail: The poll loop runs entirely inside the one COM worker thread (called from entity_trim/extend/fillet/chamfer via _run). While it sleeps/polls for up to 8s, no other COM tool call can execute (single worker). Worse, the outer _run timeout (default 60s) is independent of deadline_s (8s); if a corner op's SendCommand wedges AutoCAD at a prompt, CMDACTIVE may never reach 0, the ESC×3 + RuntimeError fires at 8s — but if AutoCAD is fully hung the GetVariable('CMDACTIVE') call itself blocks and the 60s _run timeout triggers the executor-rebuild/zombie-thread path above.

risks.title: transaction_active flag left True if commit/rollback _run raises; rollback EndUndoMark+UNDO B can leave AutoCAD mid-command
risks.detail: self._transaction_active is set to True only AFTER await self._run(_sync) succeeds in transaction_begin (line 1402), but if _run raises (timeout/COM error) the begin partially set the undo mark with no flag — and in commit/rollback the flag is cleared after _run; if _run raises, the flag stays stale True. transaction_rollback issues doc.EndUndoMark() then SendCommand('_UNDO B\n') without the CMDACTIVE guard or _safe_send_command polling used elsewhere; if a command is active this UNDO can interleave badly. No state reconciliation on reconnect after timeout (which sets _connected=False but does not reset _transaction_active).

risks.title: SelectionSets.Add(UUID) leaks if a name collides or selection set already exists from a prior crashed run; Add() failure precedes the try/finally
risks.detail: ss = doc.SelectionSets.Add(ss_name) is OUTSIDE the try block. UUID collision is improbable, but if a previous process aborted leaving an identically-named set, or if Add raises for any reason, no cleanup runs (acceptable since nothing was created). The more material issue: SelectionSets persist in the drawing for the session; the finally ss.Delete() only runs if Add succeeded. If _apoint/SelectCrossing raise (e.g. invalid coords), the finally still deletes, which is correct — but there is no SelectionSets pre-clean, so a leftover set from a hard crash (no finally executed) permanently blocks reuse of standard names and bloats the SelectionSets table until purge.

risks.title: _capture_window GDI cleanup can leak the bitmap/DC if SelectObject/CreateCompatibleDC partially fails or returns the old object
risks.detail: Cleanup deletes save_bmp.GetHandle(), save_dc, mfc_dc, hwnd_dc. But save_dc.SelectObject(save_bmp) returns the previously-selected default bitmap which is never restored before DeleteDC; selecting a bitmap into a DC and deleting the DC while the bitmap is still selected is a known GDI leak pattern on some drivers. Also if win32ui.CreateDCFromHandle succeeds but CreateCompatibleDC raises, hwnd_dc is released but mfc_dc.DeleteDC is still attempted (None-guarded, fine) — main concern is the unrestored default bitmap.

risks.title: _find_autocad_hwnd matches any visible window whose title contains 'AutoCAD', may capture a wrong/splash/secondary window
risks.detail: EnumWindows returns the first visible window with 'AutoCAD' in the title and stops. This can match a tool palette, About dialog, splash, or a different AutoCAD-branded product, producing a screenshot of the wrong window. It is not tied to the COM ActiveDocument/app HWND (AcadApplication.HWND is available and would be authoritative).

risks.title: system_run_lisp returns the SendCommand return value as 'result' but AutoCAD SendCommand returns None (no LISP value capture)
risks.detail: doc.SendCommand always returns None in the AutoCAD COM API; it does not return the LISP evaluation result. The method reports result='nil' always (str(None) is falsy→'nil'), so callers get no actual LISP return value. This is a silent correctness/UX gap: the tool advertises a 'result' that is never meaningful. No CMDACTIVE guard either, so a LISP form that opens a prompt deadlocks the next call.

risks.title: entity_offset side_x/side_y parameters are accepted but ignored; offset direction is non-deterministic
risks.detail: The signature takes side_x/side_y to pick which side to offset, but the body only calls ent.Offset(float(distance)) and never uses side params. AutoCAD's Offset with a signed distance offsets to a fixed side per entity orientation; positive/negative cannot reliably honor the caller's intended side. Result side is effectively unpredictable vs the documented intent, and diverges from any ezdxf implementation that may honor side.

risks.title: drawing_audit/transaction_rollback/drawing_undo/redo use SendCommand without the CMDACTIVE pre-guard used in system_run_command
risks.detail: system_run_command added a CMDACTIVE check (line 1473) precisely to avoid deadlocking when a command/prompt is active, but drawing_audit ('_AUDIT Y'), drawing_undo ('_UNDO 1'), drawing_redo ('_REDO'), and transaction_rollback ('_UNDO B') call doc.SendCommand directly with no such guard and no _safe_send_command polling. If AutoCAD is at a prompt, these silently misfire or leave the command line in a bad state, and the next COM call can hang until the _run timeout.

smells.title: All 8 premium meta-tools are NotImplementedError in COM backend but fully implemented in ezdxf — hard parity gap that breaks the documented premium workflow on Windows/COM
smells.detail: drawing_plan, drawing_critique, point_from_snap, construction_xline, construction_clear, drawing_apply_iso_layers, dimension_auto, entity_select_smart all raise NotImplementedError ('pending Task #7/#8/#9'). CLAUDE.md mandates these as 'non-negotiable for production engineering output' (plan-before-draw, point_from_snap, drawing_critique-then-finalize, drawing_apply_iso_layers). ezdxf_backend.py implements all of them (lines 1753-2008 verified). On the default Windows/COM backend the entire premium pipeline fails, and drawing_finalize→DrawingValidator runs against a backend that cannot critique. This is the single largest COM-vs-ezdxf parity defect.

smells.title: _batch_mode instance attribute is set/cleared but never read; only _COM_STATE['batch_mode'] is consulted by _regen
smells.detail: entity_array_rectangular/polar set both self._batch_mode and _COM_STATE['batch_mode'], but _regen() (line 181) only checks _COM_STATE.get('batch_mode'). self._batch_mode (initialized line 362) is dead — never read anywhere. Two parallel batch flags, one unused, invites drift. Also _COM_STATE is module-global mutable state shared across any ComBackend instance, conflated with the per-thread 'app' key.

smells.title: block_create_from_entities is a stub returning ok:False, diverging from the ABC contract and likely from ezdxf
smells.detail: Returns {'ok':False,'error':'... not supported in COM backend. Use system_run_command with _BLOCK instead.'} instead of implementing or raising. This is a silent capability gap vs base.py:441 abstract method; callers that branch on ok get a soft failure rather than a clear NotImplementedError, and parity with ezdxf is unverified/likely broken.

smells.title: Duplicated linetype-load logic between _ensure_linetype_loaded and linetype_load (MEASUREMENT/FILEDIA/-LINETYPE dance copy-pasted)
smells.detail: The MEASUREMENT→acadiso.lin/acad.lin selection, FILEDIA save/restore, and '_-LINETYPE _LOAD ... \n\n' SendCommand sequence are duplicated almost verbatim. Divergence risk: linetype_load verifies post-load and raises on failure; _ensure_linetype_loaded does not verify, so setting entity.Linetype after a silent load failure can still throw a less helpful COM error.

smells.title: Repeated COM property re-reads of mspace.Count / Documents.Count inside hot loops and returns
smells.detail: analysis_stats reads mspace.Count in the for-range AND again in the return dict 'total_entities' (line 1280) — two COM round-trips that can disagree if the drawing changes; entity_list/analysis_select_by_type call mspace.Item(i) per index (O(n) COM calls, each marshaled across the STA boundary) with no caching. For large drawings this is slow and the double Count read is a latent inconsistency.

smells.title: Broad 'except Exception' with debug-only logging swallows COM errors throughout, masking real failures
smells.detail: Many helpers (_entity_info type extraction, Offset indexing, ArrayRectangular iteration, GetAttributes, GetBoundingBox) catch bare Exception and log at debug level then continue/fallback, so genuine COM faults (disconnected, busy) are indistinguishable from benign 'property not supported' and never surface to the caller. The HRESULT-based reconnect logic in _run is bypassed because these inner catches eat the com_error before _run sees it.

smells.title: Stale/aspirational comments referencing 'Task #7/#8/#9' left in shipped code
smells.detail: Section header '── premium meta-tools (Task #7/#8/#9 implement these) ──' and per-method 'pending Task #N' messages are TODO breadcrumbs that have shipped. They confirm the parity gap is known but unaddressed in this backend; doc (CLAUDE.md) vs code drift since CLAUDE.md presents the premium workflow as available.

moduleMaps.notes: Verified against backends/base.py (ABC + dataclasses), backends/ezdxf_backend.py (premium meta-tools implemented at lines 1753-2008, confirming the COM parity gap), config.py:38 (COM_CALL_TIMEOUT default 60s, env-overridable), and server.py:2281-2320 (drawing_finalize calls backend.view_screenshot + DrawingValidator().run(backend) + backend.drawing_info — finalize relies on COM methods that exist, but drawing_critique used as the production gate per CLAUDE.md does NOT exist on COM). Highest-impact items: (1) the timeout path leaks a CoInitialize'd STA worker thread + COM proxy every time it fires with no CoUninitialize anywhere (com_backend.py:415-433, 68-71); (2) the 8 NotImplementedError premium meta-tools make the documented mandatory workflow unusable on the default Windows/COM backend (com_backend.py:1636-1660). I did not execute anything (no AutoCAD/Windows COM available in this audit); risks are from static reading only.

moduleMaps.purpose: Deterministic, parametric engineering-CAD primitives that back the gear_draw_*/keyway_draw_*/titleblock_apply_iso_a3/drawing_finalize/drawing_critique/drawing_plan MCP tools. It generates involute spur/helical gear outlines, DIN 6885 keyed bores and keyway sections, ISO 7200 A3 title blocks, standard engineering layer/linetype scaffolds, a closed-enum drawing critique, and an 8-step pre-completion validator. All drawing functions are async and call the abstract AutoCADBackend; pure-math helpers (involute/tooth) are sync.
