Several arXiv IDs carry 2026 dates consistent with the stated June 2026 current date.

research.topic: AutoCAD COM/ActiveX automation API surface vs. com_backend.py — under-used high-value capabilities
research.summary: com_backend.py (1661 lines) exercises only a narrow slice of the AutoCAD ActiveX object model. Every entity creation call goes through `_msp()` → `_acad_doc().ModelSpace` (com_backend.py:125-127), so the backend is effectively ModelSpace-only. The ActiveX ModelSpace/PaperSpace/Block collections expose 60+ Add* methods; the backend wires up ~12 (AddLine, AddCircle, AddArc, AddLightWeightPolyline, AddText, AddMText, AddHatch, AddSpline, AddEllipse, AddPoint, InsertBlock) plus 5 dimension adds and ArrayRectangular/ArrayPolar. The largest, highest-value gaps for an *engineering drawing* product are: (1) PAPER SPACE / LAYOUTS + PLOT — there is no layout abstraction at all; `drawing_export_pdf` (com_backend.py:528-534) blindly calls `doc.Plot.PlotToFile(path, \"DWG To PDF.pc3\")` with no PlotConfiguration / page setup / layout selection / multi-sheet publish, so it cannot honor the A3/scale targets the premium workflow (drawing_plan sheet_size/scale) promises. (2) TABLE objects (AddTable) for BOM / revision / parts tables — absent; users must hand-draw grids. (3) MLEADER (AddMLeader) for ISO leader notes — absent; CLAUDE.md's premium rules even reference \"leader notes\" but no tool creates them. (4) FIELDs — no field/title-block auto-population, so titleblock text is static. (5) 3D SOLIDS / REGIONS (AddBox/Cylinder/ExtrudedSolid/RevolvedSolid + Boolean + AddRegion) — system_status advertises \"all_entity_types\" (com_backend.py:1443) which is false. (6) XREF (AttachExternalReference). (7) DYNAMIC BLOCK properties (GetDynamicBlockProperties). (8) Named UCS (UserCoordinateSystems.Add) — needed for the construction-geometry / multi-view workflow. (9) DIMENSION STYLES (DimStyles.Add + CopyFrom) — dimensions are created with whatever the current style is; no ISO-129 dimstyle is ever set, undercutting the \"Dimensions are ISO 129\" rule in CLAUDE.md. (10) GRADIENT HATCH (AddHatch with acGradientObject) and MTEXT inline formatting. (11) Real SelectionSet filter API is used in only two places (analysis_select_by_layer com_backend.py:1333-1350, analysis_entities_in_region :1286-1308); entity_list (:967-999) and analysis_select_by_type (:1352-1367) iterate every ModelSpace item in Python instead of pushing a DXF group-code filter into AutoCAD, which the web sources flag as the #1 COM performance killer. WebSearch + WebFetch were available and used; all API signatures below are confirmed against Autodesk ActiveX reference pages.

findings.title: PaperSpace Collection — AddPViewport / Layout association (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: PaperSpace is a Block-like collection exposing AddPViewport(center, width, height) for floating viewports, plus all the same Add* geometry/annotation methods as ModelSpace, and it owns a Layout object holding every plot setting (page size, plot device, scale, plot area). Layouts collection + PlotConfiguration objects store named page setups.
findings.relevance: The backend has zero paper-space support (every create call hits `_msp()` ModelSpace, com_backend.py:125-127). To produce real plotted A3 sheets at a declared scale (the contract of drawing_plan(sheet_size,scale) and titleblock_apply_iso_a3), you must create/select a Layout, set its CanonicalMediaName + StandardScale/CustomScale, drop an AddPViewport, and lock its scale. Add a `layout_*` family (layout_create/list/set_active, viewport_create, viewport_set_scale).

findings.title: Layout / PlotConfiguration + Document.Plot.PlotToFile / PlotToDevice (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: The Plot object supports PlotToFile(filename, [printConfig]) and PlotToDevice, but correct output requires first configuring the Layout/PlotConfiguration (ConfigName, CanonicalMediaName, PlotType, PlotRotation, StandardScale, PlotWithLineweights). Multi-sheet PDF is done by iterating layouts (SetLayoutsToPlot) or via the Sheet Set / PUBLISH path.
findings.relevance: `drawing_export_pdf` (com_backend.py:528-534) calls PlotToFile with only the device string and no page setup — output paper size, scale, and which space (model vs layout) are entirely unspecified and non-deterministic. This silently ignores the scale/sheet the premium workflow set up. Fix: build a plot config (or apply a saved page setup) honoring drawing_plan's sheet_size/scale before plotting; add multi-layout publish.

findings.title: AddTable Method (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: ModelSpace/PaperSpace.AddTable(insertionPoint, numRows, numColumns, rowHeight, colWidth) returns a Table object; cells are filled with SetText(row,col,string), SetCellValue, merged, and styled via a TableStyle. Tables are first-class AcDbTable entities.
findings.relevance: Engineering deliverables need BOM / parts-list / revision tables. Currently users would have to hand-draw line grids with entity_create_line and place text cell-by-cell — error-prone and not editable. A `table_create` + `table_set_cell` tool (plus a `bom_from_blocks` meta-tool counting block refs) is a high-value, low-risk add that fits the 'meta-tools over raw' rule in CLAUDE.md.

findings.title: AddMLeader Method (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: ModelSpace.AddMLeader(pointsArray, leaderLineIndex) creates a multileader; content (MText or block) and arrowheads are set via the MLeaderStyle and the object's TextString/ContentBlockName properties. Modern ISO leader notes are MLEADER, not the legacy AddLeader.
findings.relevance: CLAUDE.md's premium dimension rule explicitly carves out 'leader notes' as a valid manual case, but no tool can create one. Adding `mleader_create(points, text, style)` lets the agent place balloon callouts / weld notes / surface-finish flags properly instead of faking them with separate line+text entities (which drawing_critique would also mis-count).

findings.title: 3D Solids: AddBox/AddCylinder/AddExtrudedSolid/AddRevolvedSolid/AddSphere/AddTorus/AddWedge + Boolean + AddRegion (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: ModelSpace exposes AddBox(center,L,W,H), AddCylinder(center,r,h), AddExtrudedSolid(region,height,taper), AddRevolvedSolid(region,axisPt,axisDir,angle), and Boolean(acUnion|acSubtraction|acIntersection) for combining; AddRegion(objectArray) builds 2D regions from closed curves. Windows-only (not LT).
findings.relevance: system_status reports capability 'all_entity_types' (com_backend.py:1443) but the backend creates no 3D solids, no regions, and no booleans — the claim is inaccurate. If 3D/MCAD output is in scope, a `solid_*`/`region_*` family unlocks an entire dimension of the product; if not, the advertised capability string should be corrected to avoid misleading agents.

findings.title: AttachExternalReference Method (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: object.AttachExternalReference(PathName, Name, InsertionPoint, XScale, YScale, ZScale, Rotation, Overlay[, Password]) attaches/overlays an xref and returns an ExternalReference object; Detach/Reload/Bind/Unload manage its lifecycle. Confirmed signature.
findings.relevance: No xref support exists (base.py has no xref_* methods). Real drawing sets reference standard borders/details. Adding `xref_attach/detach/reload/bind` lets the agent compose drawings from shared title blocks and detail libraries instead of copying geometry, and keeps file sizes small.

findings.title: GetDynamicBlockProperties / DynamicBlockReferenceProperty Object (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: BlockReference.GetDynamicBlockProperties() returns an array of DynamicBlockReferenceProperty objects, each with PropertyName, Value (settable), AllowedValues, and IsDynamicBlock on the reference. This is how you drive parametric blocks (e.g. adjustable beam length, door swing).
findings.relevance: block_insert/block_get_attributes (com_backend.py:1195-1251) only handle attribute text, not dynamic parameters. Engineering symbol libraries (valves, fasteners, structural sections) are almost always dynamic blocks. Add `block_get_dynamic_props`/`block_set_dynamic_prop` so the agent can size/configure inserted parametric blocks.

findings.title: DimStyles.Add + DimStyle.CopyFrom (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: Create a dimension style with Document.DimStyles.Add(name); set all the DIMxxx system variables on the document, then call dimStyle.CopyFrom(doc) to bake the current dim variable state into the named style. ActiveDimStyle selects it for subsequent dims.
findings.relevance: All dimension_* methods (com_backend.py:740-811) create dims under whatever DimStyle is current and never establish an ISO-129 style — directly undercutting CLAUDE.md's 'Dimensions are ISO 129' rule and dimension_auto's intent. Add `dimstyle_create_iso` (set DIMTXT/DIMASZ/DIMDEC/DIMTOFL/etc., CopyFrom, make active) and call it from drawing_apply_iso_layers so dims are standards-compliant by construction.

findings.title: Fields (AcDbField) in MText / Attributes — FieldDisplay (ActiveX/.NET)
findings.takeaway: Field expressions (e.g. %<\AcVar Filename>%, %<\AcObjProp ...>%, %<\AcDiesel ...>%) embedded in MText/Attribute strings auto-update. They live in an AcDbField in the object's extension dictionary; FieldDisplay sysvar toggles the gray background. Set via the text string itself plus a regen/UpdateField.
findings.relevance: Title-block text (date, sheet number, scale, filename, drawing area) is static today (titleblock_apply_iso_a3 just writes text). Supporting field expressions in entity_create_mtext / a `field_insert` tool makes title blocks and dynamic notes self-updating — a hallmark of professional drawings and a differentiator over hand-placed text.

findings.title: AddHatch with acGradientObject — gradient fills (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: AddHatch(PatternType, PatternName, Associative[, HatchObjectType]) accepts HatchObjectType=acGradientObject to produce a gradient; GradientName ('LINEAR','SPHERICAL', etc.), GradientColor1/GradientColor2 (AcCmColor via SetRGB), and GradientAngle control it. AppendInnerLoop adds islands.
findings.relevance: entity_create_hatch (com_backend.py:651-676) hardcodes acPatternTypePreDefined=0, single outer loop, no inner loops (islands) and no gradient. Sectional engineering hatches with holes need AppendInnerLoop; presentation/architectural fills need gradients. Extend the hatch tool with island loops + a gradient mode.

findings.title: UserCoordinateSystems.Add (named UCS) + ActiveUCS (ActiveX)
findings.takeaway: Document.UserCoordinateSystems.Add(origin, xAxisPnt, yAxisPnt, name) creates a named UCS; setting doc.ActiveUCS makes it current so subsequent coordinates are relative to it. UCSs persist in the drawing.
findings.relevance: Multi-view drawings (front/top/side, auxiliary views, section planes) are far easier to place with named UCS origins than absolute-coordinate math. drawing_plan already takes view_count; a `ucs_*` family would let each view live in its own coordinate frame, complementing point_from_snap and reducing coordinate-guessing.

findings.title: SelectionSet.Select/SelectAll with DXF group-code filters (ActiveX) + COM perf guidance
findings.takeaway: SelectionSet supports Select(mode, pt1, pt2, filterType, filterData) and SelectAll(filterType, filterData) where filterType is a short-int array of DXF group codes (0=type, 8=layer, 62=color, -4 for logical operators) and filterData the matching values — letting AutoCAD do the filtering. Web sources stress that per-entity COM property reads in a Python loop are the dominant performance cost; minimize round-trips.
findings.relevance: entity_list (com_backend.py:967-999) and analysis_select_by_type (:1352-1367) loop over every ModelSpace.Item(i) in Python and read .ObjectName/.Layer per entity — O(n) COM round-trips that get slow on large drawings. analysis_select_by_layer (:1333-1350) already shows the right pattern (group code 8). Push type/layer/color filters into SelectAll filter arrays so AutoCAD returns only matches, and back entity_select_smart with the same mechanism.

opportunities: Layout/PaperSpace family (layout_create/list/set_active, viewport_create via AddPViewport, viewport_set_scale + lock) so the A3/scale promised by drawing_plan/titleblock_apply_iso_a3 actually drives a plotted sheet — every create call currently hits ModelSpace only (com_backend.py:125-127).

Make drawing_export_pdf deterministic: configure the Layout/PlotConfiguration (CanonicalMediaName, StandardScale/CustomScale, PlotType, PlotWithLineweights) from the active PlanSpec before PlotToFile, instead of the bare `doc.Plot.PlotToFile(path,'DWG To PDF.pc3')` at com_backend.py:528-534. Add multi-layout publish (SetLayoutsToPlot / PUBLISH) for multi-sheet PDFs.
table_create + table_set_cell (AddTable) and a bom_from_blocks meta-tool, replacing hand-drawn grid lines for BOM/parts/revision tables.
mleader_create (AddMLeader) for ISO leader notes / balloons / weld & surface-finish callouts — CLAUDE.md already reserves 'leader notes' as a manual dimension case but no tool exists.

dimstyle_create_iso (set DIMTXT/DIMASZ/DIMDEC/DIMTOFL..., DimStyles.Add + CopyFrom, set ActiveDimStyle) wired into drawing_apply_iso_layers so dimension_* output actually satisfies the ISO-129 rule instead of inheriting an arbitrary current style.

Field support: allow field expressions in entity_create_mtext and/or a field_insert tool so title-block date/sheet/scale/filename/area auto-update; expose FieldDisplay toggle. Makes titleblock_apply_iso_a3 dynamic rather than static text.
xref_attach/detach/reload/bind/unload (AttachExternalReference — confirmed signature) so drawings compose from shared borders/detail libraries instead of copied geometry.

block dynamic-property access (GetDynamicBlockProperties → block_get_dynamic_props / block_set_dynamic_prop) to drive parametric symbol libraries; block_insert today only handles attribute text (com_backend.py:1195-1251).

Push selection into AutoCAD: rewrite entity_list (com_backend.py:967-999) and analysis_select_by_type (:1352-1367) to use SelectionSet.SelectAll with DXF group-code filter arrays (the pattern analysis_select_by_layer already uses at :1339) — eliminates O(n) per-entity COM round-trips on large drawings and gives entity_select_smart a real backend.
Extend entity_create_hatch (com_backend.py:651-676) with inner-loop islands (AppendInnerLoop) for sectioned parts with holes and an acGradientObject gradient mode for presentation fills.

Named UCS family (ucs_create via UserCoordinateSystems.Add + set ActiveUCS) to anchor each of drawing_plan's view_count views in its own coordinate frame, reducing absolute-coordinate math alongside point_from_snap.

3D solids/regions/booleans (AddBox/AddCylinder/AddExtrudedSolid/AddRevolvedSolid/AddRegion + Boolean) IF 3D is in scope — and either implement it or correct the misleading 'all_entity_types' capability advertised by system_status (com_backend.py:1443), which is currently false.
research.confidence: Medium-high. research.topic: ezdxf 1.3 plus 1.4 capability map versus autocad-mcp EzdxfBackend under-used features
research.summary: EzdxfBackend in backends ezdxf_backend.py is 2051 lines and uses only a thin slice of the ezdxf 1.3 plus and 1.4 API. A Grep over the backends folder for set_gradient, set_solid_fill, paperspace, add_viewport, odafc, export_dwg, groupby, dimstyles, MTextEditor, add_ordinate, and qsave returned zero matches. Key gaps with line references in backends ezdxf_backend.py. Hatch is pattern only with a single closed polyline path at lines 593 to 606. Dimensions never set a dimension style or override dict so all dims use the ezdxf default style at lines 668 to 774, and the dimension_auto ordinate style fakes it with two linear dims at lines 1988 to 2004. Rendering uses raw matplotlib with no Configuration and no SVG or PyMuPDF backend at lines 357 to 379 and 1400 to 1424. There is no paperspace or viewport support and view zoom methods are no ops at lines 1394 to 1398. DWG export is faked because drawing_save_as accepts the dwg format but just calls doc saveas writing DXF bytes at lines 345 to 355. Querying is hand rolled with Python loops instead of the ezdxf query method and groupby at lines 990 to 1011, 1293 to 1309, 1371 to 1390, and 2008 to 2050. Audit reports errors but never calls the auditor fix method and drawing_open has no recover fallback at lines 448 to 457 and 309 to 331. MTEXT uses a plain string with no MTextEditor at lines 578 to 591. Offset supports only LINE and CIRCLE at lines 868 to 908, and trim, extend, fillet, and chamfer support only LINE plus LINE at lines 1535 to 1750.

findings.title: Drawing add-on Configuration and SVG and PyMuPDF backends are unused
findings.takeaway: ezdxf addons drawing exposes MatplotlibBackend, PyMuPdfBackend, native SVGBackend, PlotterBackend, and DXFBackend. matplotlib qsave takes bg fg dpi and config. Configuration controls lineweight_scaling, min_lineweight, background_policy, color_policy, lineweight_policy ABSOLUTE or RELATIVE, hatch_policy, and line_policy. layout Page Margins and Settings set scale, fit_page, crop_at_margins, output_layers, and Units mm. draw_layout can render any paperspace layout.
findings.relevance: drawing_export_pdf lines 357 to 379 and view_screenshot lines 1400 to 1424 hardcode matplotlib with no Configuration and no paperspace. Adopting Configuration plus SVGBackend or PyMuPdfBackend gives true ISO lineweights, white backgrounds, vector output, and layout specific rendering.

findings.title: Hatch supports gradients, solid fills, edge paths, islands, and associativity
findings.takeaway: Hatch supports set_solid_fill with color style and rgb, set_gradient with color1 color2 rotation centered one_color tint and a name such as LINEAR or SPHERICAL, set_pattern_definition for custom patterns, BoundaryPaths add_edge_path with line arc ellipse and spline edges, islands via path_type_flags, and associate. ISO and ANSI pattern names work in set_pattern_fill.
findings.relevance: entity_create_hatch lines 593 to 606 only calls set_pattern_fill with one add_polyline_path. It cannot produce section view solid fills, gradient fills, curved or island boundaries, or associative hatches.

findings.title: Dimension styling via DimStyle plus per dim override dict, and ordinate and arc dims
findings.takeaway: add dim methods return a DimStyleOverride. You pass dimstyle by name and or an override dict with dimtxt dimasz dimclrt dimdec and dimlfac to control text height arrow size color precision and scale. doc dimstyles new creates reusable styles. ezdxf also provides ordinate and arc dimensions and warns it does not honor every dimension variable.
findings.relevance: Every dimension method lines 668 to 774 calls add dim with no dimstyle and no override so all dims use the ezdxf default style with wrong text height and arrow size for ISO sheets. The dimension_auto ordinate branch lines 1988 to 2004 fakes ordinates with two rotated linear dims.

findings.title: odafc add-on does real DXF to DWG conversion from R12 to R2018
findings.takeaway: ezdxf addons odafc provides is_installed, readfile, export_dwg with doc filename and version, and convert with version defaulting to R2018. It wraps the external ODA File Converter to produce true DWG and to read DWG and DXB, supporting R12 through R2018.
findings.relevance: drawing_save_as accepts the dwg format but just calls doc saveas lines 345 to 352, writing a DXF with a dwg name, silently producing a wrong format file. Wiring odafc export_dwg guarded by is_installed makes DWG export real, and odafc readfile lets drawing_open read dwg files.

findings.title: ezdxf query mini language and groupby replace hand rolled filtering
findings.takeaway: Every layout has a query method taking an entity and attribute string that supports negation, and, or, regex, and wildcard exclusion, returning an EntityQuery. groupby with a dxfattrib such as layer or a key function groups entities into a dict in one call.
findings.relevance: analysis_stats hand builds the by type and by layer dicts in a Python loop lines 1293 to 1309, analysis_select methods loop with lower case comparisons lines 1371 to 1390, and entity_select_smart pulls 5000 entities then filters in memory lines 2008 to 2050. The query method plus groupby is faster, supports compound predicates and regex, and cuts code.

research.topic: Drafting-standards conformance (ISO 128 / 129 / 13567 / 7200 / 1101, ASME Y14.5) of AutoCAD MCP Pro's engineering layer
research.summary: The project encodes the "structural" half of ISO drafting standards well but the "annotation/specification" half barely at all. ISO 128 lineweights and line types are correctly modeled: plan_spec.py defines the exact 9-value ISO 128-2 lineweight set (0.13-2.00 mm) and critique.py enforces it per-layer; layers.py provides three standard layer sets (mech/pid/iso13567) with conforming lineweights and the CENTER/HIDDEN/PHANTOM/DASHED/DASHDOT linetypes. ISO 13567 is approximated by a single hardcoded "starter" layer list (M-GEOMET-E-N style) but the field grammar (agent/element/presentation/status field codes) is not actually parsed or validated. ISO 7200 title blocks exist (titleblock.py) with most mandatory fields, but the layout geometry is hardcoded to A3 only, several text-placement constants look mis-derived, and label terminology is informal ("DWG NO" vs ISO field names). ISO 129 dimensioning is the weakest structural area: dimension_auto claims "ISO 129 dimensions" but the dimension_* backend signatures (base.py:226-257) carry NO parameter for tolerances, limit/fit deviations, or text override, so symmetric (+/-), limit, and fit-class dimensions per ISO 129-1 simply cannot be authored. ISO 1101 GD&T and ASME Y14.5 are entirely absent: there is no feature control frame, datum, or geometric-tolerance primitive anywhere in the codebase (confirmed by grep across engineering/ and backends/). The validator/critique only sanity-check geometry hygiene (fake-dim text, untrimmed corners, layer color, construction leftovers) and do not validate dimensional completeness or any tolerancing standard. Net: good for un-toleranced ISO 128/7200-style geometry on A3; not yet capable of production-quality drawings that require dimensional tolerances or GD&T.

findings.title: ISO 128-2:2020 line widths (9-value set) and narrow/wide/extra-wide groups
findings.takeaway: ISO 128-2 defines exactly 9 line widths (0.13, 0.18, 0.25, 0.35, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0, 1.4, 2.0 mm) grouped as narrow/wide/extra-wide, plus 15 numbered line patterns (clause 4.2). Width/group is chosen by drawing type, size and scale.
findings.relevance: The project already hardcodes this exact set in engineering/plan_spec.py:85 (ISO_128_LINEWEIGHTS_MM) and enforces it in engineering/critique.py:37-64 (_check_iso128). This is the project's strongest conformance point. Gap: it validates only that a layer's lineweight is IN the set, not that the right group (narrow vs wide) is used for the right element class (e.g. visible edges should be wide ~0.5, dims/center narrow ~0.18) and not the line-pattern numbering.

findings.title: ISO 129-1: dimensioning elements and tolerance indication
findings.takeaway: ISO 129-1 specifies the dimensioning elements (dimension line, value, arrowhead terminator, extension line, leader, origin symbol, centre line) and tolerance indication: symmetric tolerances use a single value preceded by +/-, and limit dimensions show explicit max and min. Dimension lines are continuous narrow lines per ISO 128-20.
findings.relevance: This is the biggest functional gap. backends/base.py:226-257 dimension_linear/aligned/angular/radius/diameter and base.py:570-578 dimension_auto have NO parameter for tolerance, limit deviations, fit class, or dimension-text override. dimension_auto's docstring claims 'ISO 129 dimensions' but cannot emit +/- symmetric, limit (max/min), or fit (e.g. H7/g6) tolerances at all. A production part drawing is impossible without this.

findings.title: ISO 13567-1:2017 layer-name field grammar (agent / element / presentation mandatory; status, sector, phase optional)
findings.takeaway: Layer names are a fixed-length, continuous alphanumeric string of mandatory fields (agent responsible, element, presentation) plus optional fields (status, sector, phase, projection, scale, work package). Short names use only mandatory fields; complex projects use more.
findings.relevance: engineering/layers.py:51-62 provides a single hardcoded ISO13567_LAYERS 'starter' list (M-GEOMET-E-N etc.) but there is no parser/validator for the field grammar, so a user-supplied layer name is never checked for ISO 13567 conformance, and the codes are mechanical-discipline-only. Opportunity: add a field-code builder/validator (agent+element+presentation[+status]) instead of a static list.

findings.title: ISO 7200:2004 mandatory data fields (8) and 180 mm title-block width
findings.takeaway: ISO 7200 defines 8 mandatory fields (Legal owner, Identification/drawing number, Date of issue, Sheet/segment number, Title, Approval person, Creator, Document type) plus 11 optional (revision index, paper size, document status, classification, responsible dept, etc.). Title block is 180 mm wide and reused across all paper sizes (A4 left margin 20 mm / right 10 mm).
findings.relevance: engineering/titleblock.py models title/drawing_no/sheet/revision/scale/material/part_no/drawn_by/checked_by/date/company and uses the correct 180 mm TB width (TB_WIDTH=180.0) and 20/10 mm margins. Gaps: (1) ISO mandatory fields 'Document type' and explicit 'Date of issue' vs informal 'DATE', and 'Creator/Approval person' labeling differ from ISO field names; (2) geometry is A3-only (SHEET_W/H=420/297 hardcoded) even though plan_spec.SheetSize allows A4-A0; (3) title X-centering at titleblock.py:171 estimates text width as len*0.3*height which will mis-center; (4) several value Y constants (e.g. tb_y0+1.5 vs value_pad_y) appear inconsistent row-to-row.

findings.title: ISO 1101 vs ASME Y14.5 GD&T: 14 symbols, feature control frames, datum reference frames
findings.takeaway: Both standards share ~14 geometric tolerance symbols and the feature-control-frame + datum-reference-frame (primary A / secondary B / tertiary C) concept. Differences: ASME Y14.5-2018 dropped Concentricity & Symmetry (use Position); ISO keeps them. ISO separates size vs form (size tolerance controls size only; envelope principle must be invoked explicitly), ASME defaults differently and states material conditions explicitly. ISO splits rules across many GPS documents; default tolerances often via ISO 2768.
findings.relevance: Entirely absent from this project: grep across engineering/ and backends/ found no feature-control-frame, datum, or geometric-tolerance primitive. No way to place a FCF, datum target, or even general ISO 2768 tolerance note. For 'production-quality engineering drawings' this is a categorical miss alongside the ISO 129 tolerance gap.

findings.title: ISO 128-20 / line conventions for section views and centre lines
findings.takeaway: ISO 128 conventions: cutting planes / section indication use long-dash-dot (phantom-type) lines with wide ends; centre lines are narrow long-dash-dot; hatching is narrow continuous at 45 degrees. Dimension and extension lines are narrow continuous.
findings.relevance: engineering/layers.py maps CENTER->CENTER linetype@0.18, PHANTOM->PHANTOM@0.18, HATCH->Continuous@0.13, which matches ISO line-class intent. engineering/section.py provides cutting_plane_line + apply_section_hatch. Gap to verify in a deeper pass: whether the cutting-plane line uses the wide-end / phantom convention and whether hatch angle/spacing follows ISO 128-50; critique.py does not check section-view line conventions.

opportunities: Add tolerance support to the dimension layer: extend dimension_linear/aligned/radius/diameter and dimension_auto (backends/base.py:226-257, 570-578) with optional params for symmetric (+/-), limit (upper/lower deviation), and ISO 286 fit-class (e.g. 'H7'/'g6') text, so dimension_auto's 'ISO 129' claim becomes real.

Implement a feature-control-frame primitive (ISO 1101 / ASME Y14.5): a gd_frame(symbol, tolerance, datums=[A,B,C], modifiers=[M/L/P]) tool plus datum-feature and datum-target symbols, rendered as MTEXT-in-boxes or true AcDbFcf where COM allows. This is the single largest missing capability for production drawings.
Add an ISO 2768 / general-tolerance title-block note option (e.g. 'ISO 2768-mK') so un-toleranced dims have a defined default, which ISO 1101 requires when no explicit GD&T is given.

Generalize titleblock.py beyond A3: derive SHEET_W/H from plan_spec.SheetSize (A4-A0) instead of hardcoded 420x297 (titleblock.py:16-17), keep the 180 mm block constant per ISO 7200, and reuse one block across sizes.

Rename title-block labels to ISO 7200 field names and add the missing mandatory fields (Document type, explicit Date of issue, Creator, Approval person), and replace the len*0.3 title-centering heuristic (titleblock.py:171) with a measured text width or a justify/anchor flag from the backend.

Turn ISO13567_LAYERS (layers.py:51-62) from a static mechanical-only list into a field-code builder+validator (agent/element/presentation[+status/sector/phase]) so arbitrary layer names can be checked for ISO 13567 conformance, and add a critique focus 'iso13567_naming'.

Extend critique.py with dimensional-completeness and ISO-129-line-class checks: flag geometry features (holes/edges) with no associated dimension, and verify dimension lines/extension lines resolve to a narrow-group lineweight; also add a section-view line-convention check (phantom cutting plane, 45-degree hatch).

Tie the ISO 128 lineweight check to element class, not just set membership: validate that visible edges use a wide group weight and dims/center/hatch use narrow, rather than only checking each layer's weight is one of the 9 allowed values (critique.py:37-64).
research.confidence: High on the codebase assessment: all four target files (engineering/layers.py, titleblock.py, validator.py, critique.py) plus plan_spec.py, __init__.py and the dimension/ISO method signatures in backends/base.py were read directly, and the absence of GD&T/tolerance support was confirmed by grep across engineering/ and backends/. WebSearch was available and used for all six standards; WebFetch was available but not needed (search snippets were sufficient and primary ISO.org pages corroborate). Medium confidence only on section.py line conventions and the exact title-block text-placement math, which were read but not exhaustively traced.

dossier:
The lineweight truncation bug is confirmed devastating: ENGINEERING_LAYERS uses floats like 0.50, 0.25, 0.18, 0.13, 0.05 mm, and `layer_create` does `int(lineweight)` (ezdxf_backend.py:1034), so every one floors to 0. Counts confirmed: 109 tools, 6 resources, 5 prompts; LICENSE/benchmarks/ci.yml all absent. All load-bearing claims verified against source. I have everything needed to synthesize.

# AutoCAD MCP Pro — Understanding & Research Dossier

## 1. Architecture at a Glance

AutoCAD MCP Pro is a **single-file FastMCP 3.0 server** (`server.py`, 2881 lines) exposing **109 `@mcp.tool` registrations, 6 `@mcp.resource` endpoints, 5 `@mcp.prompt` templates** (verified by grep, not the docs' "87"). It uses a **strategy-pattern dual backend** behind one ABC (`backends/base.py`, ~73 abstract methods + 5 dataclasses): a **live COM backend** (`com_backend.py`, pywin32, single STA-thread `ThreadPoolExecutor`) and a **headless ezdxf backend** (`ezdxf_backend.py`, file-based DXF, all sync work via `asyncio.to_thread` under one `asyncio.Lock`). Backend selection (`server.py::_make_backend`, server.py:69): env `AUTOCAD_MCP_BACKEND`; Windows `auto`/`com` tries COM then falls back to ezdxf; non-Windows always ezdxf.

Request path: `ErrorHandlingMiddleware → AuditMiddleware → TimingMiddleware → LoggingMiddleware` (server.py:197-200) → tool fn → `_backend(ctx)` (server.py:207, raises `ToolError` if backend None) → identically-named backend method → `_dc()` dataclass→dict serialization (server.py:216). Lifespan (`autocad_lifespan`, server.py:107) builds the backend singleton into `ctx.lifespan_context["backend"]`.

A **deterministic engineering layer** (`engineering/` package) backs the "premium" workflow: parametric gear/keyway/titleblock primitives, a closed-enum `drawing_critique`, an 8-step `DrawingValidator`, ISO layer/linetype scaffolds, and a `PlanSpec` plan-before-draw contract. **The central architectural fact: the premium workflow is fully implemented only on ezdxf; the COM backend stubs all 8 premium meta-tools with `NotImplementedError` (com_backend.py:1637-1660, verified) — yet COM is the default Windows backend.**

---

## 2. Per-Subsystem Summary

**`server.py` (transport + tool registry).** Thin async wrappers over the backend singleton, grouped into 13 sections (1–12 + sub-sections 8b/8c/8d batch/template/validation + 13 premium). Owns server-side composition tools (`entity_delete_many` server.py:1065, `layer_isolate` :1216, `block_find_references` :1362, `analysis_layer_stats` :1506, `entity_batch_*` :1538/:1590, `template_*` :1695/:1730, `validation_check` :1749). Entry point manually parses `--transport/--port/--host` (server.py:2866) and refuses non-loopback HTTP unless `ALLOW_REMOTE_HTTP` + `MCP_AUTH_TOKEN` (`_validate_http_bind`).

**`backends/base.py` (the contract).** `AutoCADBackend` ABC: ~73 `@abstractmethod`, 2 concrete (`set_layer_active`, `ensure_linetypes`), 5 dataclasses (`EntityInfo/LayerInfo/BlockInfo/DrawingInfo/CommandResult`), 3 helpers (`shoelace_area`, `deg2rad`, `rad2deg`). Both backends define a body for every abstract method (no structural gaps); divergences are behavioral.

**`backends/ezdxf_backend.py` (headless).** Full create/modify/query/analysis/dimension/block/layer + premium meta-tools + corner ops. Transactions = full DXF snapshot to tempfile on `_undo_stack` (trimmed to `max_undo_stack=5`, O(drawing-size) per `transaction_begin`). View ops and `system_run_command/run_lisp` are documented no-ops. Screenshot/PDF via matplotlib.

**`backends/com_backend.py` (live AutoCAD).** All COM through one STA worker with per-call timeout (`com_call_timeout`, default 60s) and executor-rebuild on timeout/fatal HRESULT. Corner ops via `_safe_send_command` (CMDACTIVE-polling). Win32 `PrintWindow` screenshots. **8 premium meta-tools + `block_create_from_entities` are stubs.**

**`engineering/` (deterministic CAD).** `gear.py` (involute spur/helical outlines), `keyway.py` (DIN 6885), `titleblock.py` (ISO 7200 A3), `layers.py` (mech/pid/iso13567 sets), `critique.py` (6 focuses), `validator.py` (8-step gate), `plan_spec.py` (PlanSpec/Issue + ISO-128 lineweight set). `section.py` (`cutting_plane_line`, `apply_section_hatch`) and `gear.generate_tooth_profile` are **dead code** (exported, zero callers/tests).

**`tests/` (184 functions, 14 files).** One ezdxf-only `backend` fixture (`conftest.py`). COM backend, server→sanitizer wiring, and `section.py` have **zero coverage**.

---

## 7. Research Synthesis

### 7a. Academic techniques (2025–2026) that validate or upgrade this design

- **GeoGramBench (ICLR 2026, arxiv.org/abs/2505.17653):** frontier LLMs score **<50%** at high-abstraction Program-to-Geometry. → Empirically justifies the repo's no-coordinate-guessing rule and `point_from_snap`/`entity_select_smart`. **Action:** expand the deterministic geometry surface (`point_intersection`, `point_offset`, `point_along`) so the LLM *never* computes coordinates.
- **Self-Improving CAD w/ FEA feedback (arxiv.org/html/2605.17448v2):** deterministic controller owns validation, LLM owns design; **+13.4pp** per feedback round; frontier models get **zero** first-attempt strict passes — *iteration beats more tokens*. → This is the repo's architecture, but `drawing_critique` is one-shot. **Action:** make it a routed repair loop; `Issue` (plan_spec.py:64-81) already carries severity/handles/detail — add per-finding repair hints.
- **CADSmith (arxiv.org/abs/2603.26512):** 5-agent Planner/Coder/Executor/Validator/**Refiner**, dual loop, exact-kernel measurements + **independent VLM judge** → Chamfer 28.37mm→0.74mm (**38×**), IoU 0.81→0.96; *removing the rendered image alone made hard cases 35× worse.* → The repo renders a screenshot at finalize (server.py:2301-2309) but only checks bytes exist (validator step 7). **Highest-leverage single upgrade: feed that render to a VLM judge + add an automated Refiner.**
- **CADFusion (ICML 2025, arxiv.org/abs/2501.19054)** & **Seek-CAD (arxiv.org/abs/2505.17702):** render is a first-class signal; step-wise visual feedback catches errors mid-build. → Add a `visual` critique focus; run critique per construction step using the existing transaction model.
- **ProCAD "Clarify Before You Draw" (arxiv.org/html/2602.03045v1):** one-round clarification → invalidity 14.6%→0.9%, Chamfer ~80% lower. → `drawing_plan` records intent passively; add a pre-plan ambiguity/conflict detector that returns targeted questions before any `entity_create_*`.
- **FutureCAD (arxiv.org/abs/2603.11831):** NL → exact B-Rep primitive grounding fixes the fillet/chamfer "which primitive" problem. → Directly targets the repo's hardest API: trim/extend/fillet/chamfer need exact handles **plus** keep_x/keep_y. NL grounding ("fillet the top-left corner") removes the burden GeoGramBench shows LLMs fail at.
- **Text-to-CadQuery (2505.06507), CAD-Coder (2505.14646), EvoCAD (2510.11631):** field converged on Python/CadQuery macros; open local VLMs read CAD images reliably; candidate-generate + critique-as-fitness raises first-try quality. → Add more parametric meta-tools (the gear/keyway pattern) and/or a sandboxed parametric-script tool; an open VLM (CAD-Coder, **Florence-2 for GD&T**, arxiv.org/abs/2411.03707) keeps the visual tier off frontier APIs.
- **MUSE (arxiv.org/html/2605.28579) + CadBench (2605.10873):** grade manufacturability/functionality, not just shape; standard metrics = volumetric/surface IoU, Chamfer, **Invalidity Ratio**. → Replace `validator.py`'s regex/title heuristics with a staged code→geometric→rubric-judge protocol; emit a single scalar "drawing score" for regression tests (the repo has *no* quantitative quality metric).

## 8. "What Would Make This the BEST AutoCAD MCP" — Prioritized Thesis

The evidence converges: **the repo is architecturally ahead on dual-backend + standards-validation but behind on the closed-loop, the production-output plumbing, and the very workflow it markets.** Priorities, evidence-backed:

**P0 — Make the marketed product actually work on its default platform.**
1. **Implement the 8 premium meta-tools on COM** (R1, com_backend.py:1637-1660). Today the CLAUDE.md-mandatory workflow 500s on every Windows install. Nothing else matters until this ships.
2. **Fix the lineweight `int()` truncation** (R2, ezdxf_backend.py:1034 → multiply mm by 100 / map to ACAD enum). This single bug nullifies the ISO-128 system *and* makes the iso128 critique a permanent no-op (R3). The standards-validation gate is the **competitive moat** (Snyk roundup; Autodesk's own flagship demo) — it must be real.
3. **Add CI + a coverage gate + the first COM smoke tests** (R6, ci.yml deleted; pytest-cov unconfigured). Restore the LICENSE file (legal drift).

**P1 — Own the moat by closing the loop (the #1 accuracy lever in every 2025–26 paper).**
4. **Turn `drawing_critique` into a routed iterative Refiner loop** with per-`Issue` repair hints and a bounded budget (CADSmith Refiner; FEA-agent +13.4pp/round; Physics-in-the-Loop 3.3–6.0 iters converge). The `Issue` dataclass already carries handles+detail.
5. **Promote the finalize screenshot to a first-class `visual` critique focus fed to a VLM judge** (CADSmith: dropping the render → 35× worse; CADFusion). Use an **open local VLM** (CAD-Coder / Florence-2 for GD&T) behind a `[vision]` extra so the headless path is unaffected. Add a **dimension round-trip check** (VLM-read dims vs created `dimension_*` entities) to catch what proximity-only `dim_overlap` (R4) cannot.
6. **Add a ProCAD-style pre-plan clarification pass to `drawing_plan`** (invalidity 14.6%→0.9%): detect missing/conflicting dims, material, tolerance, view_count-vs-intent before any geometry.
7. **Emit a scalar "drawing score" / Invalidity-Ratio** (MUSE/CadBench) so the premium workflow is regression-testable — the repo has no quantitative accuracy metric.

**P2 — Reach production-drawing parity (what un-toleranced ISO 128 can't deliver).**
8. **ISO 129 tolerances + ISO 1101/Y14.5 GD&T**: add ±/limit/fit params to `dimension_*` and a `gd_frame(symbol, tol, datums, modifiers)` + datum/ISO-2768-note primitive. This is the categorical gap blocking real part drawings; no surveyed text-to-CAD product covers 2D GD&T — a differentiator.
9. **Real plotted sheets**: COM Layout/PaperSpace/Plot-config so `drawing_plan(sheet_size, scale)` and `titleblock_apply_iso_a3` produce a correct A-series plot; on ezdxf wire `odafc.export_dwg` (kills the fake-DWG R11) and the drawing-addon `Configuration` for true-lineweight SVG/PDF.
10. **An ISO-129 `DimStyle`** created once and applied to all dims (COM `DimStyles.Add+CopyFrom`; ezdxf dimstyle overrides) so "Dimensions are ISO 129" becomes true, and **MLEADER** for the leader notes the docs already reserve.

**P3 — Catch the field on breadth (table-stakes elsewhere, absent here).**
11. **A 3D solids tier** (extrude/revolve from 2D profiles + booleans on COM; STEP/STL on ezdxf) — *or* correct the false `all_entity_types` claim (com:1443). Every competitor has 3D.
12. **Parametric editability + entity-level conversational editing** (Adam/Zoo): capture driving dims in PlanSpec, expose a re-drive tool; "select this circle → make it the 20mm bore" via existing handle model + `entity_select_smart`/`point_from_snap`.
13. **Authenticated remote HTTP** (API-key middleware) given `dangerous_commands_enabled` exists (server.py:111); a **standard-parts/symbol library** (`library_insert` over `block_insert`, dynamic-block props); **NL→B-Rep grounding** (FutureCAD) over the brittle trim/fillet/chamfer keep-point API.

**P4 — Hygiene that compounds with the above.** Generalize LINE-only corner ops + LINE/CIRCLE-only offset via the ezdxf path module; replace hand-rolled selection with `query()`/`groupby()` (ezdxf) and `SelectionSet.SelectAll` filters (COM); fix `entity_array_polar` 360° dup (R9), `dimension_angular` tx/ty (R12), the COM thread-leak `CoUninitialize` (R5); delete dead `section.py`/`generate_tooth_profile`; reconcile `system_about` catalog (R15) and the stale README/CLAUDE.md drift (§5).

**Files central to every priority:** `backends/com_backend.py:1637-1660` (P0.1), `backends/ezdxf_backend.py:1034` + `engineering/layers.py:14-26` (P0.2), `engineering/critique.py:37-64,194-197` (P0.2/P1.5), `engineering/plan_spec.py:64-87` (P1.4/P1.6/P2.8), `backends/base.py:226-257` (P2.8), `server.py:528-534/2294-2309` (P2.9).
